sources.title: LangChain Subagents Documentation
keyInsights: Supervisor pattern maintains conversation context across turns; router is stateless dispatch
Subagents are stateless - all memory maintained by main agent
Three-tool async pattern: start job, check status, get result
Context engineering levers: subagent specs, inputs, and outputs

sources.title: How we built our multi-agent research system - Anthropic
keyInsights: Lead agent decomposes queries and spawns 10+ subagents for complex research
15x more tokens than chat, but 90.2% improvement on research tasks
Save research plans to external memory before context limits
3-5 subagents spawn simultaneously, each using 3+ parallel tool calls

sources.title: Create custom subagents - Claude Code Docs
keyInsights: Subagents defined as Markdown files with YAML frontmatter
Three scopes: project (.claude/agents/), user (~/.claude/agents/), plugin
Subagents cannot spawn other subagents
Background subagents auto-deny unpre-approved permissions
Persistent memory with user/project/local scope
Worktree isolation option for code-modifying subagents

sources.title: Vercel AI SDK Subagents
keyInsights: Async generator pattern for streaming subagent progress to UI
toModelOutput decouples user-visible output from model context
Tool part states: input-streaming, input-available, output-available, output-error
Subagent tools cannot use needsApproval

sources.title: Handoffs - OpenAI Agents SDK
keyInsights: Handoffs represented as tools (transfer_to_agent_name)
Input types allow structured delegation metadata (reason, priority)
Input filters transform conversation history before receiving agent sees it
Bidirectional handoffs enable circular agent flows

sources.title: How agents call agents - DEV Community
keyInsights: Three communication directions: downward (universal), upward (divergent), lateral (rare)
Two-level hierarchies outperform flat and deeper architectures
41-87% production failure rates in multi-agent systems
Dynamic team formation outperforms static crew definitions

sources.title: What is A2A protocol - IBM
keyInsights: A2A uses Agent Cards for discovery, HTTPS + JSON-RPC 2.0 for transport
Tasks have lifecycle states: submitted, working, input-required, completed, failed
SSE streaming for real-time progress updates
A2A complements MCP: MCP for tools, A2A for agent-to-agent

sources.title: Top 10 Agent Frameworks 2026
keyInsights: LangGraph uses DAGs, CrewAI uses role-based delegation, AutoGen uses conversational loops
CrewAI introduced Agent Operations Platform for visual workflow design
AutoGen supports recursive group chats with unlimited nesting
Hybrid approaches combining frameworks outperform pure approaches

sources.title: Understanding Multi-AI Agent Patterns
keyInsights: ReAct pattern for adaptive workflows, SPA pattern for safety-critical ops
State synchronization failures when agent handoffs misalign
Explicit task contracts and typed interfaces essential for reliability

sources.title: Claude Code Sub-Agent Parallel vs Sequential Patterns
keyInsights: Three execution patterns: parallel (3+ independent), sequential (dependent), background (non-blocking)
CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL env var controls sub-agent model
Most sub-agent failures stem from poor invocation, not execution
Include specific scope, file references, and success criteria in spawn

sources.title: Agent Orchestration: When to Use Which Framework
keyInsights: Evolution from chains (2023) to agentic RAG (2024) to multi-agent orchestration (2025)

sources.title: Agentic Imperative: Crew AI & Semantic Kernel
keyInsights: CrewAI hierarchical mode spawns a special manager agent

sources.title: Microsoft AutoGen: Orchestrating Multi-Agent LLM Systems
keyInsights: AutoGen uses assistant-user agent pairs for collaborative task completion

sources.title: Announcing the Agent2Agent Protocol - Google
keyInsights: A2A enables any-to-any agent communication across frameworks

sources.title: A2A Protocol GitHub Repository
keyInsights: Open protocol spec with reference implementations

sources.title: Building a LangGraph multi-agent orchestrator - Reddit
keyInsights: User never talks directly to sub-agents; only through orchestrator

sources.title: OpenClaw: Difference between agents and sub-agents
keyInsights: Sub-agents get agents.md and tools.md, not full context; go away when session ends

sources.title: Introduction to subagents - Anthropic Skilljar
keyInsights: Structured output formats, obstacle reporting, and limiting tool access for reliability

sources.title: Sub-agent delegation pattern issues - Claude Code GitHub
keyInsights: Sub-agents cannot call other sub-agents despite config claims - known limitation

sources.title: Generative UI: The AI agent is the front end
keyInsights: Agents paint interactive UI components on demand instead of pre-built interfaces

sources.title: Building ClaudeClaw: OpenClaw-Style Agent System
keyInsights: Each subagent gets its own context window, tool set, spawned via Agent/Task tool

sources.title: Delegating Tasks Between Agents Using Handoffs - CodeSignal
keyInsights: Handoff is a transfer of responsibility from one agent to another
